title: Mr. Rime Battle Guide
summary: An [Ice]/[Psychic] utility attacker that clears oppo[sing] screens on entry and abuses [freeze-dry] for 4x damage into [Water] pivots.
weaknesses: Base 70 Speed needs [cho[ice]-scarf] to matter offensively

recommendedMoves.reason: Hits [Water] types for 4x super effective damage

recommendedItems.reason: Patches the base 70 Speed tier for [revenge] kills

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mr. Rime is an [Ice]/[Psychic] special attacker whose niche in Champions MA is built on two unusual tools: [freeze-dry] and the [screen-cleaner] ability. The 80/75/110 offensive line is workable, but the real job is bringing a turn-one answer to opposing screen leads while still threatening [Water] pivots the [rest] of the tier can't touch.

## Strengths
[screen-cleaner] is the headline — simply switching Mr. Rime in wipes any active [light-screen], [reflect], or [aurora-veil] and torches a screen team's entire plan for zero cost. [freeze-dry] is the secondary hook: it hits [Water] types super effectively, meaning any Gyarados, Quaquaval, or Palafin switch-in walks into a 4x multiplier. 110 SpA is already enough neutral damage, and one [nasty-plot] turn puts it at realistic wallbreaker numbers.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Ice]/[Psychic] stacks 2x weaknesses to [Steel], [Fire], [Rock], [Dark], and [Ghost], meaning Kingambit, Gholdengo, Ceruledge, and most Mega Fires turn Mr. Rime into chip. Base 70 Speed is the other issue — without choice-scarf it cannot [revenge] kill, and [close-combat] from a faster Fighter deletes it from 80/75. The raw type chart does a lot of work against this pokemon.

## Recommended Moveset
[freeze-dry] is the locked STAB and the single reason Mr. Rime is on the team. [psychic] provides the [Psychic] STAB for neutral coverage, and [focus-blast] covers [Dark] and [Steel] walls the STAB combo cannot touch. The last slot is [nasty-plot] so that after a [screen-cleaner] entry Mr. Rime can set up in the same turn the opponent is scrambling to re-build screens. For Trick Room teams, swap in [trick-room] and run a quiet nature.

## Item · Ability · Tera
choice-scarf is the default item because base 70 Speed desperately needs the patch. [focus-sash] is the alternative if the job is purely to guarantee the [screen-cleaner] entry. The ability must be [screen-cleaner] — [ice]-body does nothing in MA. Tera [Water] erases the [Fire], [Rock], and [Ice] weaknesses, and Tera [Fairy] flips the [Dark] matchup while powering up no STAB but handing a clean defensive profile.

## Teammates & Synergy
Mr. Rime pairs best with fast cleaners that want screens gone — Dragapult, Meowscarada, Garchomp all benefit from the turn-one screen removal. [Fighting] and [Ground] partners cover the [Steel] and [Dark] cores that wall the set, and an [intimidate] ally softens physical priority. A slow pivot that can bring Mr. Rime in safely (U-turn from a bulky [water]) is ideal because its middling Speed cannot force entries on its own.

## Play Patterns
Send Mr. Rime in the moment an opposing lead commits to [light-screen] or [aurora-veil] — the entry alone wins the trade. Click [freeze-dry] only when a [Water] switch-in is confirmed, otherwise [psychic] is the safer neutral move. Stack [nasty-plot] only when the opponent is forced to sacrifice or swap. Save Tera for the specific turn a [Fire] or [Dark] move is obvious; Tera [Water] or [Fairy] that turn resets the matchup.
